channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Kaine and Cao face off in only debate of campaign for US Senate seat from Virginia
item.description: U.S. Sen. Tim Kaine of Virginia and Republican challenger Hung Cao have gone toe to toe in their only debate of the campaign over issues from illegal immigration to tariffs on foreign goods.
item.dc:creator: Ben Finley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mayorkas warns FEMA doesn't have enough funding to last through hurricane season
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas says the agency tasked with helping communities recover from Hurricane Helene and other natural disasters does not have enough money to make it through the hurricane season.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking And Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cook County State's Attorney candidate Former Judge Eileen O’Neill Burke on crime, guns and youth violence
item.description: In deep blue Cook County, the Democratic candidate for states attorney, retired Judge Eileen O’Neill Burke, is looking past the November election and sounding like she’s already on the job.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Biden won't support a strike on Iran nuclear sites as Israel weighs response to Iran missile attack
item.description: JOINT BASE ANDREWS, Md. (AP) — President Joe Biden said Wednesday he will not support an Israeli strike on sites related to Tehran’s nuclear program in response to Iran's missile attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meet the candidates running for San Diego City Attorney
item.description: There are only two candidates in the running to become San Diego City Attorney after incumbent Mara Elliott ends her last term in office in December.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: LIVE: 22nd Congressional District Debate
item.description: The two candidates seeking election to California's 22nd Congressional District will face off tonight.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, NewsNation

item.title: Some of the most notable quotes from the JD Vance-Tim Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The first and only vice presidential debate between Ohio Senator JD Vance and Minnesota Governor Tim Walz featured an often orderly, policy-focused and even civil dialogue between the two men seeking to serve as the next president's second in command.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump downplays troop injuries in 2020 missile strike: 'You mean because they had a headache?'
item.description: "So first of all, injured. What does injured mean? Injured means — you mean because they had a headache? Because the bombs never hit the fort," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Are convicted felons allowed to vote in Illinois?
item.description: With just five weeks until the 2024 presidential election, most U.S. citizens over the age of 18 are eligible to vote as long as they are registered. However, for some individuals, the right to vote may be more complicated due to a criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: The poll offers much better news for Trump than last month’s poll from Quinnipiac, which found Harris leading Trump by 3 points.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Education a potential flashpoint in Vance-Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Education hardly came up in last month’s Trump-Harris presidential debate, but it could be a central focus at Tuesday’s undercard event.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. Fish and Wildlife signs Tamarac co-stewardship agreement with White Earth
item.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service and the White Earth Nation have signed a co-stewardship agreement for the Tamarac National Wildlife Refuge in northwestern Minnesota. The agreement will not change public access to the refuge, but will give tribal resource managers more input in refuge management.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gunderson, MPR News

item.title: Habitat for Humanity volunteers continue Carter’s legacy on 100th birthday
item.description: For 40 years, the project has brought together volunteers, celebrities and elected officials to build homes and highlight the need for affordable housing.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Senate Democrat calls for Congress to return to pass disaster aid after Hurricane Helene
item.description: Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) on Tuesday called for the Senate to return from its lengthy preelection recess to pass a disaster aid package following the damage left in multiple states by Hurricane Helene. “I think we should pass it yesterday.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Reviewing the Vance-Walz vice-presidential debate
item.description: MPR News host Angela Davis talks with political analysts about Tuesday night’s vice-presidential debate between Ohio Sen. JD Vance and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: Harris, Trump woo Gen Z voters in critical swing states
item.description: The Harris and Trump campaigns are stepping up their outreach to Generation Z voters in critical battlegrounds, targeting a voting bloc expected to play a key role in a tight White House race where any marginal advantage gained could make the difference.
item.dc:creator: Yash Roy, NewsNation

item.title: A century of Jimmy Carter: Longest-living former president turns 100
item.description: In 1998, at the ripe age of 74, former President Carter put some of his thoughts about getting older to paper. "The virtues of aging," Carter wrote in a book that year, "include both the blessings that come to us as we grow older and what we have to offer that might be beneficial to others.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Florida Republican 'absolutely certain' there will be supplemental to address Helene aid
item.description: Rep. Kat Cammack (R-Fla.) said Monday she’s “absolutely certain” Congress will consider a supplemental aid package to provide additional disaster relief to areas devastated by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: How young Republicans and Democrats are whipping up votes in key districts
item.description: As the presidential race ramps up in Georgia, one vital voting demographic is mobilizing and hoping to impact the race: young people.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly, Erika Ryan, Kira Wakeam, and Courtney Dorning, MPR News

item.title: Which candidates most closely align with your views? Take our quiz to find out
item.description: Election Day 2024 in Minnesota is Tuesday, Nov. 5. Before you cast your vote, take a closer look at the candidates on your ballot with our candidate quizzes.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Johnson, MPR News

item.title: PIX11 News to host NY-18 debate with candidates Pat Ryan and Alison Esposito
item.description: September 30, 2024, New York, NY – PIX11 News today announced it will host a live debate for New York’s 18th Congressional District. Democratic incumbent Congressman Pat Ryan, a retired military intelligence officer in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: PIX 11
